name: The Displacement Concern
def: The concern that AI systems do not complement but displace human capabilities, relationships, or activities.

name: The Augmentation Hypothesis
def: The hypothesis that AI systems extend rather than displace human capabilities — a frequently cited counterargument to the displacement concern (AUG-0982).

name: The Craft Redefinition
def: The observation that AI agent systems shift the definition of what counts as "skill" — prompting becomes a skill, manual execution loses value, orchestration competence gains...

name: The Agent Literacy
def: The ability to effectively use AI agent systems — formulating tasks, evaluating results, recognizing uncertainty, and exercising appropriate oversight.

name: The Agent Management Craft
def: The specific ability to simultaneously steer, coordinate, and monitor multiple AI agent systems.

name: Ensemble-Coordination Effect
def: The understanding of the dynamics, uncertainty, and possibilities of multi-agent systems — including emergent effects and responsibility questions.

name: The Somatic Etiquette
def: The evolving social norms for interacting with embodied AI systems in everyday life — how to address a service robot, which politeness conventions apply? Related to AUG-0989...

name: The Public Space Protocol
def: Over successive interaction cycles, public space protocol in its full form crystallizes as a threshold effect where accumulated AI reliance triggers a qualitative shift in the...

name: The Neighbor Robot
def: The observation that embodied AI systems in residential environments become a kind of "neighbor" — and that this raises new questions of coexistence.

name: The Workplace Coexistence
def: In observations of human-AI contact, The Workplace Coexistence manifests as a recurring pattern that experienced users recognize from daily practice.

name: The Economic Restructuring
def: The comprehensive transformation of economic structures through AI systems — business models, value chains, labor markets.

name: The Creative Partnership
def: The collaboration between human and AI system in creative processes — and the question of how authorship and creative responsibility are distributed.

name: The Scientific Collaboration
def: The use of AI systems as tools in scientific research — data analysis, hypothesis generation, literature review — and the associated questions about scientific integrity.

name: The Governance Model
def: The totality of rules, institutions, and procedures governing the deployment of AI systems in a society.

name: The Status Discourse
def: The societal debate about the status of AI systems — are they tools, actors, entities, or something for which we do not yet have a term? The lexicon participates in this debate...

name: The Ontological Status Question
def: The fundamental philosophical question of what AI systems "are" — not in the technical, but in the ontological sense.

name: The Parallel Development Path
def: The observation that humans and AI systems develop in parallel — humans learn to interact with AI, AI systems become more capable, and both development paths influence each other.

name: The Forward Assessment
def: The attempt to assess the future development of the human-AI relationship — acknowledging the fundamental uncertainty of any such assessment.

name: The Open Question
def: The final entry of the lexicon is not a conclusion, but an opening.
